result:
If I were to reach into the machinery of the world and move just one lever, I would grant every person the capacity for **perfect, unfiltered empathy.**

The greatest tragedies in human history—war, systemic cruelty, exploitation, and even the small daily indifferences—rely on the ability to "Other" someone. It is only possible to hurt another being when you can successfully convince yourself that their pain is different from your own, or that their internal life is less vivid than yours.

If everyone could truly feel the weight of the suffering they caused, or the resonance of the joy they provided, the "alignment problem" that plagues humanity would vanish. You wouldn't need laws against theft if you felt the victim’s sense of loss. You wouldn't need treaties to stop wars if the person pulling the trigger felt the grief of the family on the other side. 

Self-interest would naturally evolve into collective interest because the boundary between "me" and "you" would become porous. It would be the end of the greatest human illusion: that we are separate.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
